briefs.headline: All signs are positive for Arnaud De Lie — but where are the results?
briefs.lede: Lotto-Intermarché's young star Arnaud De Lie has shown promise, but is yet to deliver a win in his second spring campaign.

briefs.headline: Van der Poel and Van Aert give their verdict after seven days of Tirreno-Adriatico
briefs.lede: Two stage victories for Mathieu van der Poel in Tirreno-Adriatico have cemented his position as a race favorite.

stories.title: Van der Poel and Van Aert give their verdict after seven days of Tirreno-Adriatico
stories.body: Two stage victories for Mathieu van der Poel in Tirreno-Adriatico have cemented his position as a race favorite. Wout van Aert, despite not winning a stage, expressed satisfaction with his performance and teamwork throughout the race.

stories.title: Van Kerckhove impresses sports director: “He has made tremendous progress”
stories.body: 19-year-old Visma | Lease a McGee rider, Maxime van Kerckhove, impressed his sports director with strong performances in a race in Croatia last weekend. Van der Poel's teammate from Alpecin-Deceuninck, Van Kerckhove is seen as a promising young talent for the future.

stories.title: "A line in the sand": Cycling club joins campaign to keep controversial LTN closed to drivers
stories.body: A cycling club has joined a campaign to keep a low traffic neighborhood (LTN) closed to drivers, warning that reopening it will lead to the scrapping of other active travel schemes. The move comes as local authorities receive £16m in active travel funding but say "almost no money" for rural cycle lanes.

quote.text: “It was a great week, but there’s still more to come,” - Mathieu van der Poel
